title: MEG-to-East Asia VLCC voyages displayed a rebound in March
section_path: MEG-to-East Asia VLCC voyages displayed a rebound in March
section_path_text: MEG-to-East Asia VLCC voyages displayed a rebound in March
summary:
Main
This week, in the East, we discuss the reasons behind the muted activity on TD3C and divergent MR and LR2 behaviour on the East/West routes. Over in the West, we focus on the role that a prospective sanctioning of Venezuela could play in the freight market, and we examine the drivers behind the recent surge of Russia diesel at sea. By Ioannis Papadimitriou
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2024-04-05 094908.png | assets/2024-04-05_v3sg748q9111vsjah6q53tu01gypjw_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_7aed1d297631.png
Image reference: 1.png | assets/2024-04-05_v3sg748q9111vsjah6q53tu01gypjw_img_1_0ef2bf6038c2.png
MEG-to-East Asia VLCC voyages displayed a rebound in March, indicating a bounce back of enquiries from the East although loadings still remain below 2023 averages.
